sentence: Subjective:

HPI: Jacqueline reported that she injured her right ankle approximately 3 years of mountain biking experience, specifically during a mountain biking lesson, where she fell and twisted her ankle outward.
She described the pain as being located on the outer ankle, with some numbness and tingling in her toes and chest.
She denied any fever or chills, but reported taking Tylenol for pain, which did not provide relief.
She has a history of taking Tylenol for pain, and denied any other medical conditions or allergies.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: Tylenol [dosage not specified] [frequency not specified] [indication not specified]
Objective:
Vital signs: Temperature 98.6°F, Blood pressure 118/77, Heart rate 77 beats per minute.
Physical exam: On the right ankle, there is some edema and erythema on the lateral malleolus, associated ecchymosis and a few excoriations.
There is pain to palpation of the right lateral malleolus, decreased flexion and extension of the right ankle, as well as internal and external rotation.
The knee and foot appear to be intact.
Test Results: X-ray of the right ankle shows no acute fracture or bony abnormality, but rather some soft tissue injury.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I believe Jacqueline has an acute right ankle sprain.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; right; active.
Plan:
I prescribed Meloxicam 15 milligrams once a day for pain management.
I recommended icing the area for 20 minutes, 5 times a day, and weight-bearing as tolerated.
I anticipate that Jacqueline will start feeling better in the next couple of weeks, and instructed her to return for follow-up if she does not improve.
I also ordered an air cast and provided crutches for support.
